title: Squad Git Note (v1)
description: Schema for a single JSONL entry in a refs/notes/squad/* blob. Notes are JSONL: one JSON object per line, no wrapping array.

v.description: Schema version. Increment on breaking changes. Readers should skip entries with v > their supported version.

agent.description: Agent role. Must match the namespace suffix: refs/notes/squad/{agent}

instanceId.description: Agent role + machine identifier. Format: {agent}-{machineId}. Used for deduplication and tracing only. Example: data-CPC-tamir-WCBED

sessionId.description: The Copilot CLI session ID that produced this note. UUIDv4 or the session identifier from squad-monitor.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. When this note was written. Used as part of the deduplication key.

commitSha.description: Full 40-character SHA of the commit this note is attached to. Never short SHA.

type.description: Note type determines promotion eligibility. decision/finding → promoted. context/assessment/summary → archived only.

summary.description: One-line summary. ≤120 chars. This is what appears as the header in decisions.md.

reasoning.description: Full reasoning. May be multi-paragraph. Explain the WHY, not the WHAT. The WHAT is in the commit diff.

alternatives.description: What was NOT chosen, and why. Empty array if no alternatives were considered.

confidence.description: Agent's confidence in this note. high → Tier 1 promotion eligible. medium → Tier 2. low → archive only.

severity.description: For type=finding only. critical/high → Tier 1 promotion. medium/low/info → archive only.

promotionCandidate.description: Explicit promotion flag. Set to true to request Tier 1 promotion regardless of confidence. Use sparingly — agent-flagged promotions are the highest-trust signal.

prNumber.description: GitHub PR number. Null if commit is not (yet) in a PR.

workItemId.description: Azure DevOps work item ID. Null if not linked.

relatedCommits.description: Full SHAs of other commits this decision touches. For context navigation.

supersedes.description: Timestamp of the note entry this supersedes. Points to the timestamp field of the earlier entry in the same namespace+commit. If set, Ralph will not promote the older entry.

tags.description: Freeform lowercase tags. Used by Ralph for categorization. Examples: auth, jwt, performance, database, security

content.summary: Use JWT for auth middleware on new /api/reports endpoint
content.reasoning: Existing auth.go already uses JWT (lines 47-89). Adding API key strategy would require refactoring the auth interceptor and introducing a strategy selector. JWT is already tested and working. The marginal security benefit of API keys for this internal endpoint doesn't justify the refactor risk.
alternatives: API key auth — rejected: requires auth interceptor refactor, no measurable security benefit for internal endpoint
No auth — rejected: out of scope, violates security policy

content.summary: JWT secret sourced from env var — ensure KeyVault rotation is configured
content.reasoning: The JWT secret in auth.go line 48 is read from JWT_SECRET env var. If this value is rotated in KeyVault, the running service will not pick it up until restart. This is not a vulnerability in this commit, but an operational risk. The existing KeyVault integration (keymanager.go) supports dynamic reload — auth.go should use that.
